 {"id":"bd-389n","title":"Implement FrameBudgetConfig with sensible defaults","description":"# Implement FrameBudgetConfig with sensible defaults

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). Configuration struct for tuning budget behavior per-application.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Frame budget configuration
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct FrameBudgetConfig {
    /// Total frame budget (default: 16ms for ~60fps feel)
    pub total: Duration,
    
    /// Budget reserved for diff computation
    pub diff_budget: Duration,
    
    /// Budget reserved for ANSI emission
    pub present_budget: Duration,
    
    /// Whether to skip frames when severely over budget
    pub allow_frame_skip: bool,
    
    /// Minimum frames between degradation level increases
    /// Prevents rapid oscillation
    pub degradation_cooldown: u32,
    
    /// Minimum frames at a level before upgrading
    /// Ensures stability before returning to higher fidelity
    pub upgrade_threshold: u32,
    
    /// Whether to log budget overruns
    pub log_overruns: bool,
    
    /// Show visual indicator when degraded (helpful for debugging)
    /// When enabled, shows small icon in corner during degradation
    pub show_degradation_indicator: bool,
    
    /// Callback for degradation events (for telemetry/monitoring)
    pub on_degradation: Option<Box<dyn Fn(DegradationLevel, DegradationLevel) + Send + Sync>>,
    
    /// Cost estimates for common operations
    pub cost_estimates: CostEstimates,
}

/// Estimated costs for operations (for should_degrade checks)
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CostEstimates {
    /// Cost of Unicode box-drawing borders
    pub unicode_border: Duration,
    /// Cost of styled text rendering  
    pub styled_text: Duration,
    /// Cost of animation tick
    pub animation_tick: Duration,
}

impl Default for FrameBudgetConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(16),
            diff_budget: Duration::from_millis(2),
            present_budget: Duration::from_millis(4),
            allow_frame_skip: true,
            degradation_cooldown: 3,
            upgrade_threshold: 5,
            log_overruns: true,
            show_degradation_indicator: false,  // Off by default in production
            on_degradation: None,
            cost_estimates: CostEstimates::default(),
        }
    }
}

impl FrameBudgetConfig {
    /// Create config for high-performance mode (8ms budget)
    pub fn high_performance() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(8),
            degradation_cooldown: 2,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    /// Create config for low-latency mode (aggressive degradation)
    pub fn low_latency() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(8),
            degradation_cooldown: 1,
            upgrade_threshold: 10,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    /// Create config for development (shows indicator, verbose logging)
    pub fn development() -> Self {
        Self {
            show_degradation_indicator: true,
            log_overruns: true,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
}
```

## User Experience Features

### Degradation Indicator
When `show_degradation_indicator: true`, a small icon appears in the corner:
- `[!]` when in SimpleBorders or NoStyling
- `[!!]` when in EssentialOnly or Skeleton
- Hidden when at Full quality

### Degradation Callback
For monitoring/telemetry, applications can register a callback:
```rust
config.on_degradation = Some(Box::new(|old, new| {
    metrics::counter!("render.degradation").increment(1);
}));
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All config fields with sensible defaults
- [ ] Preset configurations (high_performance, low_latency, development)
- [ ] Optional degradation indicator
- [ ] Optional callback for degradation events
- [ ] All fields documented
- [ ] Unit tests for defaults and presets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.638388803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:14:39.665804684Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:14:39.665775318Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - FrameBudgetConfig with total, phase_budgets, allow_frame_skip, degradation_cooldown, upgrade_threshold","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-389n","depends_on_id":"bd-1iqn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-38d17","title":"Diagram Minimap for Large Graph Navigation","description":"## Goal
For diagrams with 20+ nodes, render a small minimap (8-12 cells tall) in a corner showing the entire diagram at tiny scale using Braille dots. A rectangular viewport indicator shows which portion is currently visible.

## Why
- Large diagrams are the primary case where terminal rendering struggles — 80×24 shows maybe 8-10 nodes at readable size.
- Without a minimap, users lose spatial orientation when panned/zoomed into a large graph.
- Minimaps are a well-understood UX pattern from code editors, games, and CAD tools.
- The Canvas/Braille system is perfect: at 2×4 dots per cell, a 20×8 minimap covers 40×32 "pixels" — enough to show topology of a 50-node graph.

## Implementation
1. Compute minimap scale: full diagram bounds / minimap area dimensions.
2. Render nodes as filled Braille dots (small filled rectangles at minimap scale).
3. Render edges as Braille lines between node centers.
4. Overlay viewport rectangle (highlighted border) showing currently visible region.
5. Position minimap in configurable corner (default: bottom-right).
6. Toggle with 'm' key.
7. Click on minimap to jump viewport to that position (if mouse support available).

## Design Decisions
- Minimap always uses Braille mode (even if main diagram uses cell mode) for maximum information density.
- Node labels are NOT shown in minimap — just dots for topology.
- Selected node (from navigation feature) highlighted in minimap with accent color.
- Minimap has a subtle border/background to distinguish from main diagram.

## Dependencies
- Canvas rendering infrastructure (bd-ukp1f)
- Viewport/zoom math (already partially in MermaidShowcase)

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add render_minimap method)
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/mermaid_showcase.rs (minimap toggle + positioning)

## Tests
- Unit: minimap scale calculation for various diagram sizes
- Snapshot: 20-node diagram with minimap visible at 120×40
- Unit: viewport indicator position matches actual viewport","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"EmeraldBasin","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:28:01.638550157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:14:30.711174017Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:14:30.711145924Z","close_reason":"Completed: Braille minimap overlay with 11 tests, integrated into showcase","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","interactivity","mermaid"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38d17","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.286279635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-38f8","title":"Harness: Theme Cycling + Themed Chrome","description":"## Background

`ftui-harness` is the **PRIMARY reference application** (per README), but it currently doesn't showcase FrankenTUI's built-in theme system (multiple coherent palettes, alpha-aware overlays, semantic tokens).

The demo-showcase already demonstrates Ctrl+T theme cycling via `ftui-extras::theme`; the harness should too so the first-run experience is visually impressive and consistent across both demos.

## Scope

- Make `ftui-harness` depend on `ftui-extras` with the `theme` feature enabled.
- Add `Ctrl+T` hotkey to cycle the active theme (`ftui_extras::theme::cycle_theme`).
- Apply theme tokens consistently across harness chrome:
  - Frame background fill (e.g., `bg::DEEP`)
  - Panels/blocks use alpha surfaces (`alpha::SURFACE` / `alpha::OVERLAY`) to demonstrate compositing
  - Foregrounds use semantic tokens (`fg::PRIMARY`, `fg::MUTED`)
  - Status line includes current theme name + key hints
- Ensure theme change is immediate and does not require restart.

## Acceptance Criteria

Checklist:
- [ ] `ftui-harness` includes `ftui-extras` `theme` and compiles in default features.
- [ ] `Ctrl+T` cycles through built-in themes and updates the UI immediately.
- [ ] Harness chrome (background/panels/status line) uses theme tokens rather than hard-coded colors.
- [ ] Alpha blending is exercised: at least one panel uses a semi-transparent background over a different base background.
- [ ] Snapshot/E2E evidence: at least one deterministic artifact proves theme cycling works.

## Testing Plan

- Unit tests:
  - Theme cycling wraps correctly (`N` themes, `N+1` returns to first).
  - Style invariants (fg/bg present for status line style).
- Snapshot tests (if harness has them): add/extend snapshots for at least 2 themes.
- If no snapshot infra exists for harness: add a minimal deterministic PTY E2E check that asserts:
  - theme name changes in status line after `Ctrl+T`
  - no panics

## E2E Plan

Extend an existing harness E2E script or add a small PTY-driven test run that:
- launches `ftui-harness` in a PTY at fixed size
- sends `Ctrl+T` twice
- captures frames or stdout artifacts
- logs verbose JSONL: env/capabilities, timings, seed, and the observed theme names

## Alien Artifact Upgrades

- Color rigor: treat palette selection as a constraint satisfaction problem in perceptual space (OKLCH) and enforce WCAG AA in tests.
- Graceful degradation: if alpha is unsupported (future), fall back deterministically to opaque surfaces without breaking layout.

## Extreme Optimization

- Theme switching must be O(1) per frame (no per-frame palette rebuilds). Cache any derived styles and validate via microbench if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"GrayCompass","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:10:21.514287698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:57:47.879699087Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:57:47.879678358Z","close_reason":"Completed: harness theme cycling coverage + E2E conflict cleanup + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","harness","polish","theme"],"comments":[{"id":401,"issue_id":"bd-38f8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting implementation: wire ftui-extras/theme into ftui-harness (Cargo deps), add Ctrl+T to cycle themes, and apply theme tokens + alpha surfaces to background/panels/status line. Will add at least one deterministic PTY/E2E artifact or minimal unit coverage.","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:10:45Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-397","title":"Create adversarial tests for escape injection","description":"TESTING - ADVERSARIAL SECURITY TESTS

## Background
FrankenTUI must be secure against untrusted content. Escape sequences in user-provided text could corrupt the terminal or execute malicious sequences. This implements ADR-006 (Sanitize-by-Default).

## Threat Model

### Attack Vectors
1. **Log injection**: Untrusted output contains escape sequences
2. **Title injection**: OSC sequences to change window title
3. **Clipboard injection**: OSC 52 to set clipboard
4. **Cursor corruption**: CSI sequences to move cursor
5. **Mode hijacking**: Enabling raw mode, mouse, etc.

### Example Malicious Inputs
- "\x1b[2J" - Clear screen
- "\x1b[H" - Home cursor
- "\x1b]0;pwned\x07" - Set window title
- "\x1b]52;c;SGVsbG8=\x07" - Set clipboard
- "\x1b[?1049h" - Enter alt screen
- "\x1b[?1000h" - Enable mouse
- "\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m" - Color injection

## Technical Requirements

### Sanitizer Implementation
pub fn sanitize_for_display(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no control characters
    if !input.bytes().any(|b| b < 0x20 || b == 0x7F || b == 0x1B) {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    
    // Slow path: filter control sequences
    let mut output = String::with_capacity(input.len());
    let mut chars = input.chars().peekable();
    
    while let Some(c) = chars.next() {
        match c {
            '\x1b' => skip_escape_sequence(&mut chars),
            c if c.is_control() && c != '\n' && c != '\t' => {
                // Replace with visual representation
                output.push_str(&format!("^{}", (c as u8 + 64) as char));
            }
            _ => output.push(c),
        }
    }
    
    Cow::Owned(output)
}

### Test Categories

#### 1. Escape Sequence Filtering
- [ ] ESC [ ... (CSI) filtered
- [ ] ESC ] ... (OSC) filtered
- [ ] ESC P ... (DCS) filtered
- [ ] ESC _ ... (APC) filtered
- [ ] ESC ^ ... (PM) filtered

#### 2. Control Character Handling
- [ ] NUL (0x00) filtered
- [ ] BEL (0x07) filtered
- [ ] BS (0x08) filtered
- [ ] Tab (0x09) preserved
- [ ] LF (0x0A) preserved
- [ ] CR (0x0D) handled
- [ ] DEL (0x7F) filtered

#### 3. Incomplete Sequence Handling
- [ ] Truncated ESC at end
- [ ] Truncated CSI at end
- [ ] Truncated OSC at end
- [ ] Invalid sequence bytes

#### 4. Raw Passthrough (Explicit Opt-In)
- [ ] raw_text() API exists
- [ ] Loud documentation warning
- [ ] Works for ANSI colors when intended

## PTY Tests

### Test Script
for payload in "${MALICIOUS_PAYLOADS[@]}"; do
    # Run payload through ftui log viewer
    echo "$payload" | ftui-test-harness
    
    # Verify terminal state unchanged
    verify_terminal_state
    
    # Verify no escape sequences in output
    verify_no_escapes
done

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ESC/CSI/OSC/DCS/APC filtered by default
- [ ] Control characters filtered except tab/newline
- [ ] Fast path for clean input (no allocation)
- [ ] Raw passthrough API documented with warnings
- [ ] PTY tests verify terminal state preserved
- [ ] Fuzz testing for parser robustness

 {"id":"bd-39c5f","title":"Fix truncate_legend_text to use display width + O(1) constraint lookup","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:17.808280919Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:26.304079801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:26.304047670Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mermaid"],"comments":[{"id":813,"issue_id":"bd-39c5f","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Fixed by EmeraldBasin. Two bugs from GoldBear's deep review: (1) truncate_legend_text used chars().count() instead of visual_width() for CJK/wide chars; now uses grapheme_width() per character + Unicode ellipsis. (2) apply_order_constraints used O(n) Vec::contains(); now uses O(1) boolean array. All 505 diagram tests pass. Commits: 07a396eb, 6abbc10a.","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:26Z"}]}
